params.name: prefix
params.description: Specifies a string to filter results based on an ID prefix.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

params.name: next_token
params.description: Indicates where to start paging for queries that support pagination.

params.name: per_page
params.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

params.name: filter
params.description: Specifies the expression used to filter the results.

items.docs: Lists all known jobs in the system registered with Nomad. Supports prefix-based filtering and pagination via next_token and per_page query parameters.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Registers a new job or updates an existing job. The job must be provided as a JSON payload in the request body.
Parses a HCL or JSON job specification and returns the equivalent JSON job structure. This endpoint can be used to validate job specifications without registering them.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

params.name: next_token
params.description: Indicates where to start paging for queries that support pagination.

params.name: per_page
params.description: Maximum number of results to return per page.

items.docs: Returns a list of jobs with their current status information including allocation summaries and deployment status.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Returns the full specification and status of the specified job.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

params.name: purge
params.description: If true, the job is purged from the system and cannot be recovered. Defaults to false.

params.name: global
params.description: If true, deregisters the job in all federated regions.

items.docs: Deregisters a job and stops all allocations associated with it. Optionally purges the job from the system entirely.

info.name: Create a new evaluation for a job

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Creates a new evaluation for the given job. This can be used to force run the scheduling logic if necessary.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Invokes a dry-run of the scheduler for the job which will determine the effects of an update. The plan will not result in any changes to the cluster.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

params.name: all
params.description: If true, includes allocations from all namespaces.

items.docs: Returns a list of allocations belonging to the specified job.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Returns a list of evaluations belonging to the specified job.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Returns a list of deployments belonging to the specified job.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

params.name: diffs
params.description: If true, includes the diff between consecutive job versions.

items.docs: Returns a list of versions for the specified job, with the most recent version first.

info.name: Revert job to an older version

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Reverts the job to an older version by creating a new version with the specification of the older version.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Sets the stability of a job version, marking it as stable or unstable. Stable versions are not eligible for automatic garbage collection.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Returns a summary of the specified job including the number of allocations in each state per task group.

params.name: jobID
params.description: The ID of the job.

params.name: namespace
params.description: The target namespace. Defaults to the default namespace.

items.docs: Dispatches a new instance of a parameterized job. The job must be of type parameterized.

items.docs: Returns a list of allocations associated with the specified deployment.

params.name: deploymentID
params.description: The ID of the deployment.

items.docs: Marks the specified deployment as failed. This causes Nomad to stop the deployment and revert to the previous stable version of the job if configured to do so.

params.name: deploymentID
params.description: The ID of the deployment.

items.docs: Pauses or resumes a rolling deployment. While paused, no new allocations will be placed for the deployment.

params.name: deploymentID
params.description: The ID of the deployment.

items.docs: Promotes canary allocations in the specified deployment, moving them to the main allocation set.

info.name: Create a new evaluation for a node

params.name: nodeID
params.description: The ID of the node.

items.docs: Creates a new evaluation for the specified node. This can be used to force a re-evaluation of the node and its allocations.

params.name: nodeID
params.description: The ID of the node.

items.docs: Enables or disables drain mode on the specified node. When draining is enabled, no further allocations will be assigned to this node, and existing allocations will be migrated.

params.name: nodeID
params.description: The ID of the node.

items.docs: Purges a node from the system. The node must be in a down state.
